case.title: TIMKEN ROLLER BEARING CO. v. PENNSYLVANIA R CO

opinion.h: TIMKEN ROLLER BEARING CO. v. PENNSYLVANIA R CO, 273 U.S. 665 (1927)
TIMKEN ROLLER BEARING COMPANY, plaintiff in error, v. The PENNSYLVANIA RAILROAD COMPANY,*
Thomas P. GOODBODY, as Receiver of the Hydraulic Steel Company, plaintiff in error, v. The PENNSYLVANIA RAILROAD COMPANY.
Supreme Court of the United States
opinion.p: Messrs. Luther Day and Rufus S. Day, both of Cleveland, Ohio (Donald W. Kling, of Cleveland, Ohio, of counsel), for plaintiff in error.
Messrs. Andrew Squire, Thomas M. Kirby and Andrew P. Martin, all of Cleveland, Ohio, for defendant in error.

Dismissed for lack of jurisdiction in this Court on the authority of Transportes Maritimos Do Estado v. Almeida, 265 U.S. 104, 105, 44 S. Ct. 449, and Oliver American Trading Co. v. United States of Mexico, 264 U.S. 440, 442, 44 S. Ct. 390.
[Footnote *] Dismissal set aside 274 U.S. 181, 47 S. Ct. 550, 71 L. Ed. -.[ Timken Roller Bearing Co v. Pennsylvania R Co 273 U.S. 665 (1927) ]
